# ElGamal Encryption in Rust

Here's a complete implementation of the ElGamal encryption algorithm in Rust:

```rust
use num_bigint::BigUint;
use num_traits::{One, Zero};
use rand::RngCore;

pub struct ElGamal {
    p: BigUint,  // Prime modulus
    g: BigUint,  // Generator
    public_key: BigUint,  // Public key
    private_key: BigUint, // Private key
}

impl ElGamal {
    // Generate a random prime number of specified bit length
    fn generate_prime(bits: usize) -> BigUint {
        use num_prime::PrimeGenerator;
        let mut rng = rand::thread_rng();
        let mut prime = BigUint::zero();
        loop {
            let mut bytes = vec![0u8; bits / 8];
            rng.fill_bytes(&mut bytes);
            bytes[0] |= 1; // Ensure odd number
            prime = BigUint::from_bytes_be(&bytes);
            if prime.is_prime(10) {
                break;
            }
        }
        prime
    }

    // Generate a random number in range [1, n-1]
    fn random_in_range(n: &BigUint) -> BigUint {
        let mut rng = rand::thread_rng();
        let mut bytes = vec![0u8; (n.bits() + 7) / 8];
        let mut result;
        loop {
            rng.fill_bytes(&mut bytes);
            result = BigUint::from_bytes_be(&bytes);
            if result < *n && !result.is_zero() {
                break;
            }
        }
        result
    }

    // Initialize ElGamal with prime p and generator g
    pub fn new(p: BigUint, g: BigUint) -> Self {
        let mut rng = rand::thread_rng();
        let private_key = Self::random_in_range(&p);
        let public_key = g.modpow(&private_key, &p);
        
        ElGamal {
            p,
            g,
            public_key,
            private_key,
        }
    }

    // Encrypt a message (as BigUint)
    pub fn encrypt(&self, message: &BigUint) -> (BigUint, BigUint) {
        let k = Self::random_in_range(&self.p);
        let c1 = self.g.modpow(&k, &self.p);
        let c2 = (message * self.public_key.modpow(&k, &self.p)) % self.p;
        (c1, c2)
    }

    // Decrypt a ciphertext (c1, c2)
    pub fn decrypt(&self, c1: &BigUint, c2: &BigUint) -> BigUint {
        let s = c1.modpow(&self.private_key, &self.p);
        let s_inv = s.modinv(&self.p).unwrap();
        (c2 * s_inv) % self.p
    }

    // Get public key
    pub fn public_key(&self) -> &BigUint {
        &self.public_key
    }

    // Get prime modulus
    pub fn prime(&self) -> &BigUint {
        &self.p
    }

    // Get generator
    pub fn generator(&self) -> &BigUint {
        &self.g
    }
}

// Example usage
fn main() {
    // Create a small example (in practice, use much larger primes)
    let p = BigUint::from(23u32);  // Small prime for demonstration
    let g = BigUint::from(5u32);   // Generator
    
    // Initialize ElGamal
    let elgamal = ElGamal::new(p, g);
    
    println!("Prime p: {}", elgamal.prime());
    println!("Generator g: {}", elgamal.generator());
    println!("Public key: {}", elgamal.public_key());
    
    // Encrypt a message
    let message = BigUint::from(15u32);
    println!("\nOriginal message: {}", message);
    
    let (c1, c2) = elgamal.encrypt(&message);
    println!("Ciphertext (c1, c2): ({}, {})", c1, c2);
    
    // Decrypt the message
    let decrypted = elgamal.decrypt(&c1, &c2);
    println!("Decrypted message: {}", decrypted);
    
    // Verify correctness
    assert_eq!(message, decrypted);
    println!("\n✓ Encryption/Decryption successful!");
}

// Additional example with larger numbers
fn example_with_larger_numbers() {
    println!("\n--- Larger Numbers Example ---");
    
    // In practice, use much larger primes (1024+ bits)
    let p = BigUint::from_str_radix("
